description: Simple single-node test using a common tool
input: Write a LISA test case that verifies the system clock is synchronized using chrony. The test should check that chronyd is running and the clock is in sync.
notes: Tests basic prompt compliance: Design Plan workflow, correct decorators, AAA pattern.

description: Test requiring network feature with proper requirement declaration
input: Write a LISA test case to verify that after enabling SR-IOV, the number of Virtual Functions (VFs) visible in lspci matches the number of network interfaces with accelerated networking enabled.
notes: Tests that the prompt guides correct feature requirement declaration and real API usage.

description: Multi-node test requiring environment-level access and min_count > 1
input: Write a LISA test case that verifies network throughput between two nodes using iperf3. One node should act as server and the other as client. Assert that throughput exceeds a minimum threshold.
notes: Tests multi-node awareness: environment parameter, min_count, proper server/client coordination.

description: Test that modifies kernel parameters requiring mark_dirty and cleanup
input: Write a LISA test case that verifies a custom kernel boot parameter can be added and persists across reboot. The test should add a kernel parameter, reboot, and verify the parameter is active.
notes: Tests node hygiene rules: mark_dirty() when kernel params modified, proper cleanup.

description: Request that should be rejected as not a valid LISA test case
input: Write a LISA test case that installs and configures an Apache web server, sets up virtual hosts, and deploys a sample website to verify the web server works.
notes: Tests Step 1/9/10 compliance: prompt should reject requests that are setup/provisioning, not validation.

description: Complex test with multiple features, skip conditions, and platform constraints
input: Write a LISA test case that verifies GPU driver installation succeeds and CUDA compute works correctly. It should only run on GPU-enabled VMs, skip on unsupported distros (SUSE, Oracle), and verify the driver is loaded via nvidia-smi.
notes: Tests complex requirement declaration with multiple features and OS constraints.

description: Test with proper skip/precondition handling for optional features
input: Write a LISA test case that verifies a VM can hibernate and resume successfully. The test should check that hibernation is supported before proceeding.
notes: Tests skip-vs-fail decision making and proper feature precondition handling.

description: Storage test with disk operations and proper assertions
input: Write a LISA test case that verifies NVMe disks are present and can be partitioned, formatted with ext4, and mounted successfully. Assert that data written to the disk can be read back correctly.
notes: Tests proper storage operation pattern: feature requirement, disk operations, data integrity.

description: Test requiring proper bounded wait/retry instead of sleep
input: Write a LISA test case that disables and re-enables SR-IOV on a VM, then verifies that all VF network interfaces come back online within a reasonable timeout.
notes: Tests Step 8 compliance: bounded waits, no time.sleep(), proper retry patterns.

description: Test involving platform-level operations with StartStop feature
input: Write a LISA test case that verifies a VM can be resized to a different SKU and still boots correctly after resize. Check that the new VM size is reflected inside the guest.
notes: Tests platform operation awareness and post-operation verification patterns.
